cards.back: A statistical concept stating that the distribution of sample means approaches a normal distribution as the sample size increases, regardless of the original distribution of the data.
A range of values within which a population mean is likely to lie, with a specified level of confidence, given a sample of data.
A measure of the amount of variation in a sample mean, calculated as the standard deviation of the sample divided by the square root of the sample size.
A statistical measure used to compare the value of a data point to the mean of a dataset, calculated as (value - mean) / standard deviation.
A range of values within which a population mean is likely to lie with 95% confidence, assuming a normal distribution and a sample size of at least 30.
